title: Data Analytics Specialist – 12 months contract
description:
Job Overview:
We are seeking a skilled Data Analytics Specialist to support our program workstreams by transforming complex data into actionable insights. The ideal candidate will have strong expertise in data analysis, visualization, and reporting, with the ability to influence strategic decision-making and collaborate with stakeholders across all levels.

Key Responsibilities:

Data Analysis & Reporting:

  • Track key performance indicators (KPIs) and benchmarks across program workstreams.
  • Perform in-depth data analysis to generate actionable insights, reports, and visualizations.
  • Design and develop dashboards using tools such as Tableau, Power BI, and Excel to support data-driven decision-making.
  • Ensure data quality and accuracy in all reporting; implement and maintain data validation processes.
  • Establish and maintain reporting best practices and documentation.
  • Propose and implement automation solutions where applicable.
  • Provide training and guidance on dashboard and reporting usage.

Stakeholder & Program Support:

  • Collaborate with workstream leads and stakeholders to understand reporting requirements and metrics.
  • Present insights and recommendations to stakeholders to support strategic decision-making.
  • Act as a key point of contact for reporting-related queries within program workstreams.

Qualifications & Experience:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Business Analytics, Statistics, or a related field.
  • 2–4 years of hands-on experience in data analytics.
  • Proven experience with visualization tools (Tableau, Power BI, Excel).
  • Familiarity with statistical analysis techniques.
  • Understanding of data quality frameworks.
  • Experience with KPIs and reporting in a corporate or program environment.
  • 3–5 years of experience in program or project management is desirable.
  • Strong analytical mindset with the ability to translate complex data into compelling insights.
  • Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.

Skills & Competencies:

  • Advanced Excel and data visualization skills
  • Analytical thinking and problem-solving
  • Attention to detail and accuracy
  • Ability to work independently and collaboratively
  • Strong presentation and communication skills

Salary : S$5,000.00 - $6,000.00

       Location : West (Mapletree Business Park) (Nearest MRT Station : Labrador)

Interested applicants, please submit your application to Teresa.chan@persolapac.com

We regret to inform that only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

PERSOL Singapore Pte Ltd EA License No. 01C4394 I RCB No. 200007268E EA Reg. No. R1107631

Our full Privacy Policy is available at https://www.persolsingapore.com/policies. If you wish to withdraw your consent, please drop us an email sg.dataprotection@persolapac.com to let us know. Please feel free to contact us if you have any queries

postedCompany.description:
PERSOL is Asia-Pacific’s leading Staffing and HR solutions partner, operating across 13 markets with deep local insight and regional scale. With more than 80 offices and decades of experience, we deliver integrated workforce solutions that are tailored, tech-enabled, and designed for the dynamic world of work.

We combine human expertise with smart technology to help organisations solve workforce challenges, unlock potential, and stay ahead of change. From recruitment and talent management to workforce strategy and advisory, our collaborative approach puts your goals at the centre.

In 2025, we came together under the PERSOL name — reflecting our bold vision for the future of work and our Group’s Vision: Work and Smile.

Whether you’re building teams, growing careers, or transforming how work gets done, we’re here.
